raw_output: {"excerpt":"In general, patent growth has been high in all applications since 2014. However, over the last three years there have been diverging growth trends with very high growth rates in both smaller application areas such as agriculture and energy management and large fields such as life sciences, security and physical sciences/engineering. In contrast, the number of patent families has stagnated or even declined in certain application areas such as telecommunications, military, arts and humanities or industrial property/law/social and behavioral sciences (Figure 27). Patents in the use of GenAI in energy management have seen the largest growth, both since 2018 and also in the last three years, with applications in agriculture also growing more in the last three years."}

parsed_output.excerpt: In general, patent growth has been high in all applications since 2014. However, over the last three years there have been diverging growth trends with very high growth rates in both smaller application areas such as agriculture and energy management and large fields such as life sciences, security and physical sciences/engineering. In contrast, the number of patent families has stagnated or even declined in certain application areas such as telecommunications, military, arts and humanities or industrial property/law/social and behavioral sciences (Figure 27). Patents in the use of GenAI in energy management have seen the largest growth, both since 2018 and also in the last three years, with applications in agriculture also growing more in the last three years.

raw_output: {"M":"CAGR\\% = \\left(\\frac{V_{end}}{V_{start}}\\right)^{\\frac{1}{(\\text{end\\_year}-\\text{start\\_year})}}-1 \\times 100, where V_{start} and V_{end} are the values (e.g., number of patent families) at the start and end years respectively","T":"The CAGR percent represents the average yearly change in the number of patent families for a given entity over the specified period, expressed as a percentage; a positive value indicates growth, while a negative value indicates a decline."}

parsed_output.M: CAGR\% = \left(\frac{V_{end}}{V_{start}}\right)^{\frac{1}{(\text{end\_year}-\text{start\_year})}}-1 \times 100, where V_{start} and V_{end} are the values (e.g., number of patent families) at the start and end years respectively
parsed_output.T: The CAGR percent represents the average yearly change in the number of patent families for a given entity over the specified period, expressed as a percentage; a positive value indicates growth, while a negative value indicates a decline.

raw_output: {"M":"CAGR = \\left(\\frac{V_{end}}{V_{start}}\\right)^{\\frac{1}{\\text{end\\_year}\\,-\\,\\text{start\\_year}}}\\!-\\!1\\;\\times\\;100\\%\\,\\text{, where }V_{start}\\text{ and }V_{end}\\text{ are the numbers of patent families at the start and end years respectively}", "T":"For each entity mentioned in the source text, identify the start year, end year, and the corresponding numbers of patent families (or other metric) at those years, then calculate the compound annual growth rate (CAGR) over the interval and express it as a percentage in a table with columns entity, start_year, end_year, and cagr_percent."}

parsed_output.M: CAGR = \left(\frac{V_{end}}{V_{start}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\text{end\_year}\,-\,\text{start\_year}}}\!-\!1\;\times\;100\%\,\text{, where }V_{start}\text{ and }V_{end}\text{ are the numbers of patent families at the start and end years respectively}
parsed_output.T: For each entity mentioned in the source text, identify the start year, end year, and the corresponding numbers of patent families (or other metric) at those years, then calculate the compound annual growth rate (CAGR) over the interval and express it as a percentage in a table with columns entity, start_year, end_year, and cagr_percent.

raw_output: {"M":"CAGR = ((V_end / V_start)^(1/(Y_end - Y_start)) - 1) * 100","T":"Extract each entity mentioned in the text, determine its start year, end year, and the corresponding values, then compute the compound annual growth rate (CAGR) as a percentage using the formula and present the results in a table with columns entity, start_year, end_year, cagr_percent."}

parsed_output.T: Extract each entity mentioned in the text, determine its start year, end year, and the corresponding values, then compute the compound annual growth rate (CAGR) as a percentage using the formula and present the results in a table with columns entity, start_year, end_year, cagr_percent.
